# Experimental study of inequality and unpredictability in an artificial cultural market

**Authors:** Matthew J. Salganik, Peter Sheridan Dodds, Duncan J. Watts  
**Year:** 2006  
**URL:** https://collaborate.princeton.edu/en/publications/experimental-study-of-inequality-and-unpredictability-in-an-artif/  
**Evidence type:** Controlled online experiment

## Claim supported

Visible popularity information increases inequality and unpredictability of success.

## Key result

Increasing social influence increased inequality and unpredictability; success was only partly determined by quality.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
